description: Safety Reporting (SMS) — voluntary safety reporting system with anonymous submission, trend analysis, ASAP and NASA ASRS integration

hard_stops.label: Accident Reported — NTSB Notification Required
hard_stops.logic: Report describes an accident as defined by 49 CFR 830 — must be routed to NTSB, cannot be handled as internal safety report only

hard_stops.label: Criminal Activity Indicated
hard_stops.logic: Report content indicates potential criminal activity — must be escalated outside voluntary safety reporting program

soft_flags.label: Safety Trend Detected
soft_flags.logic: Three or more similar reports within 90 days — pattern may indicate systemic hazard requiring SMS review

soft_flags.label: Reporter Requests Follow-Up
soft_flags.logic: Reporter has opted to receive follow-up on their submission — assign to Safety Manager queue

soft_flags.label: Severe Hazard Classification
soft_flags.logic: Report hazard classified as severe — elevated priority for Safety Manager review

soft_flags.label: Near-Miss Pattern Emerging
soft_flags.logic: Multiple near-miss reports with common factors suggest an emerging safety pattern
